name: Oscar Wilde
abstract: Oscar Fingal O'Fflahertie Wills Wilde (16 October 1854 – 30 November 1900) was an Irish author, poet and playwright. After writing in different literary styles throughout the 1880s, he became one of the most popular and influential dramatists in London in the early 1890s.

works_created.t: Lady Windermere's Fan, A Play in Four Acts
The Happy Prince and Other Tales: The Selfish Giant
Guido Ferranti: Scene [Beatrice reveals to her lover, Guido, that she has murdered the duke, her husband.]
The Importance of Being Earnest, A Trivial Comedy for Serious People
The Happy Prince and Other Tales: The Happy Prince

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Dobbins
about_quotes.evidence: Like Oscar Wilde said if you tell people the truth you’d better be able to make them laugh or they will kill you.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Dobbins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012
